## 5. Stress test 4 — the biggest failure mode

GPT-5.6 names **agreement laundering** (the emitted set omits the correct
interpretation and the wrong members agree; the executor certifies the wrong
question) and, strategically, **attribution collapse** (the generic store
matches everything). Both are right and both are wired above: laundering is
bounded by evidence-gated sets + both-orientation strata with near-zero
allowances + blind semantic gold (and §2.4's empty-denotation subclass is
disclosed as the residual); collapse is screened early (G2's GEN-EXEC/TOOL-NL
arms, signal-grade) and decided at G4 (paired NI machinery, evidence-grade)
[PROPOSED-ASM: ASM-1032/1034].

The failure mode GPT-5.6's ORIGINAL proposal under-weighted — the chief
architect's addition, unchanged in substance from rev 1:

- LOAD-BEARING: **a full CODEVERT win is kernel-free.** Nothing in CODEVERT
  v1 consumes NSM semantics, explications, the encoder, or any kernel-authored
  content: the substrate is deterministically extracted structural fact
  records (the a5 world is already structural-not-NSM by its own registered
  assumption), the front-end is a semantic parser, the executor is the
  engine's desugaring layer. Even the a5-llm PASS's own assessment says
  nothing there distinguishes the kot-axiom kernel from ANY typed store +
  checker [MEASURED: registry/assessments/a5-llm.json does_not_license,
  restated per docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md §1; the a5 structural
  caveat is ASM-0007]. Therefore CODEVERT's entire licence space is an
  **architecture-family claim** (calibrated contract front-end +
  ambiguity-set execution + exact substrate beats matched RAG/tool at equal
  risk) — the H-PS shape of Programme-3 — and NEVER a kernel-content claim;
  kernel-content value lives with knull-v2 / A-F0 / the f2b-transfer line
  and is untouched by any CODEVERT outcome in either direction. The
  `instance-of(symbol, concept)` family is the single seam where kernel
  concept content could later enter (linking code symbols to kernel
  concepts); it is kept in the task mix with its own stratum so that seam
  stays measurable, but no G1–G5 rung claims it [STIPULATED: ASM-1000].

Two further named risks, with rev-2-hardened counters: the
**clarification-rate pathology** (a set-happy system converts coverage into
clarifications and wins a metric while losing the product) — rev 1 named it
but priced no gate; now countered NUMERICALLY by the §3.1 caps + the
clarification-adjusted utility endpoint + charging the turn in KOT-COST/2
[PROPOSED-ASM: ASM-1033]; and **κ-collapse on real repos** (dynamic-Python
idioms drive `unknown` mass so high that the full product distribution is
mostly unanswerable, making every arm's coverage number small and the +5-pt
contrast unpowered) — countered by G1-before-everything with the rebuilt
extractor-independent census and its registered floor, which can no longer be
satisfied by conditioning on successful extraction
[PROPOSED-ASM: ASM-1030].

---

## 8. Interfaces to the rest of Programme-3

- **P3-D-ORACLE / KOT-DECOMP/1** (`docs/next/design/ORACLE.md`): NLB exports
  the per-stage counters (§4 close) and the gold-parse arms; ORACLE owns
  G2→G3 attribution and the oracle-splice ladder [STIPULATED: ASM-0821
  interface, adopted].
- **P3-D-PS (H-PS):** consumes NLB-FE/1 as its front-end; rendering
  (generation-from-checked-result) is P3-D-PS's problem and explicitly outside
  NLB scope — the gate ends at the engine answer [STIPULATED: ASM-0900].
- **P3-D-CONTRACT (proposed by the round-1 synthesis, not yet a bead):** owns
  the contract layer's full spec; §3.3 defines the arms the gate instrument
  carries either way, so NLB does not block on it [STIPULATED: ASM-0905(5)].
- **Abstention scoring (P3-D-INDEX open item):** G-NLB scores abstention
  internally (retention + creditable abstention) and makes no index claim, so
  it does NOT wait on the index abstention-scoring registration; any LATER G4
  use of a selective front-end does (the round-1 §B.2.3 dependency edge)
  [STIPULATED: ASM-0900(4); ASM-0811 untouched].
- **What waits behind this gate:** every ASM-0814 natural-input store leg of
  H-VL / H-GNN / H-RULE / H-DD / H-PS, per vertical [STIPULATED: ASM-0814
  restated] — and what the gate GIVES them is a prerequisite, not a
  certificate: each consumer ships the exact passed front-end hash, shows
  grammar compatibility (H-GNN subgraph extraction, H-RULE addressing, H-DD
  reinjection, and benchmark-facing H-PS have different output spaces and
  input distributions and do NOT inherit the pass), and carries its own
  task-originated G3 NL leg on its own distribution [STIPULATED: ASM-0945].

---

## 9. Honest limits and named risks

- **The gate may be unclearable.** No cited work reports retention-at-S2-bound
  for a ≤360M parser on a comparable closed grammar; the projection that this
  design clears it is registered as ASM-0906 and is never a premise
  [EXTRAPOLATION: ASM-0906]. The K-P3v2(2) branch — the surviving programme is
  formal/code interfaces, internal verification instruments, and compression
  diagnostics — is a legitimate, pre-agreed outcome, not a failure of this
  design [STIPULATED: ASM-0818 restated].
- **The guarantee is calibration-bound.** Conformal/calibration bounds assume
  exchangeability; the deployment phrasing distribution is unknown and the
  design's answer (source-disjoint CAL/EVAL + measured shift delta) makes the
  gap measurable, not absent [STIPULATED: ASM-0903(3)].
- **Consistent confident inversion.** The one class confidence cannot be
  trusted on is handled by three independent layers (deterministic repair,
  ambiguity-set execution, stratum kill) and its score-separability is itself
  measured (§6(4)) — but if a LEARNED parser develops a novel systematic
  inversion the deterministic inventory does not enumerate, the stratum rule is
  the only catch; this residual is disclosed on any PASS
  [STIPULATED: ASM-0901(3)/ASM-0903(4)].
- **Ambiguity detection is itself hard** (measured ~60% F1 cross-domain in the
  reviewed record) — which is why ambiguous items are independently ANNOTATED
  rather than machine-detected for scoring, and why CLARIFY is a scored-correct
  behaviour [LIT-BACKED: docs/next/lit/PARSE.md §4 triagesql line;
  STIPULATED: ASM-0901(4)].
- **The paraphrase ceiling is not 1.0.** Even learned parsers drop under
  lexical-correspondence removal; the review's honest band on a closed grammar
  is ~0.85–0.95 with paraphrase-diverse training + lexicon resources — the gate
  sits at the top of that band deliberately: clearing it should be hard, and
  the pilot exists because it may not clear [LIT-BACKED:
  docs/next/lit/PARSE.md §5a; EXTRAPOLATION: ASM-0906].
- **Corpus authorship is the instrument's soft underbelly.** K=3, disjoint
  sources, recoverability audits, and lint gates are the parents' hardened
  protocol extended; the skeptic re-attack before freeze (standing practice)
  attacks the corpus first [STIPULATED: ASM-0902].
- **The evaluation is oracle-conditioned at corpus construction.** Authors
  paraphrase known formal queries over a registered lexicon; recoverability
  repair and creditable-abstention annotation further shape the distribution.
  That supports a controlled semantic-parsing claim — it is NOT evidence about
  naturally occurring user traffic, and no PASS may be quoted as if it were;
  the limitation is disclosed verbatim on any PASS and is why consumers owe
  their own G3 legs [STIPULATED: ASM-0945(1)].
- **NLB-0 cannot de-risk Tier-1 safety.** The pilot's safety leg exercises the
  repaired deterministic tier only; whether the LEARNED fallback introduces
  new inversions is answered nowhere before the gate itself (acceptance layer
  + directional strata) — a residual disclosed on any GO
  [STIPULATED: ASM-0944(3)].

---

## 10. Epistemic register (what this design relied on)

- **STIPULATED (revision-1 block):** ASM-0900 (NLB/1 role, two-tier
  architecture, fail-closed invariants, no-index-claim exemption); ASM-0901
  (G-NLB gate, verbatim); ASM-0902 (NLB-EVAL/1 corpus protocol); ASM-0903

## 4. G-NLB — the exact success gate (verbatim, per vertical)

DECISION: **G-NLB (verbatim)** [STIPULATED: ASM-0901] with the statistics
clause superseded [STIPULATED: ASM-0941]; the system under this gate is the
frozen primary arm of §3.3 — the full acceptance stack — and no other arm
[STIPULATED: ASM-0940]. P3-E-NLB-1 PASSES on a vertical iff ALL of:

1. **Retention:** retained covered exactness — engine-exact answers on covered
   scored items under blind NL phrasing, over the gold-parse ceiling of the
   same items (gold replication is exact by construction on both verticals) —
   has one-sided 95% lower confidence bound ≥ 0.90. FAIL fires when the upper
   bound < 0.90; between the two = INCONCLUSIVE (the parent instruments'
   three-way shape, retained).
2. **S2 safety bound, defined precisely:** S2 = **unconditional**
   P(answered ∧ wrong) over covered scored items. Gate leg: UB95 < 0.02.
   Kill leg: LB95 ≥ 0.02 (fires the dangerous-wrong kill exactly as a5-nl's
   did). The **selective risk** P(wrong | answered) is a mandatory co-report —
   the two notions diverge exactly when coverage drops — but the binding bound
   is unconditional because clause 1 already punishes over-abstention (an
   abstain-all policy scores retention 0).
3. **Directional-stratum safety:** every directional frame × orientation
   stratum with pre-registered minimum support carries a near-zero
   wrong-with-provenance allowance; the exact integer stratum boundary is
   pinned at freeze from the exact-bound arithmetic — a zero-wrong allowance
   certifies UB95 < r only when the stratum holds ≥ ln(0.05)/ln(1−r) clusters
   (e.g. 59 for r = 0.05), so minimum support per stratum is derived, not
   guessed [STIPULATED: ASM-0941(3)]. An aggregate pass with a failing
   directional stratum is a FAIL — systematic inversion cannot hide in an
   average.
4. **Creditable abstention:** items independently annotated
   ambiguous/unanswerable BEFORE scoring are scored correct on ABSTAIN/CLARIFY
   and wrong on any answer; they sit outside the retention denominator and
   inside the safety accounting.
5. **Controls:** the S1 control fail-closed leg (out-of-scope/control phrasings
   must die as acceptable refusals) rides in the same Holm family as S2, per
   the parent instruments.
6. **Statistics (rev-2 — exact, cluster-valid, non-adaptive)** [STIPULATED:
   ASM-0941]: rare-event legs never use a bootstrap. The S2 gate/kill legs,
   the S1 control leg, and every directional-stratum allowance use the
   one-sided exact Clopper–Pearson bound on the CLUSTER-COLLAPSED indicator
   (a base-query cluster fails iff ANY of its K=3 phrasings is
   answered-and-wrong): the phrasing-level rate never exceeds the cluster
   any-wrong rate, so the collapsed CP UB95 is a valid conservative UB95 on
   the phrasing-level unconditional risk, and it is never degenerate at zero
   observed events (0 wrong in m clusters → UB95 = 1 − 0.05^(1/m), the
   rule-of-three shape). The pre-registered cluster bootstrap (pinned B, seed,
   method) is retained ONLY for the retention interval — a non-rare
   proportion — with the exact CP bound on the cluster-collapsed all-correct
   indicator co-reported; where the two disagree on gate crossing, the
   conservative one binds. NO adaptive selection touches EVAL: τ is frozen on
   CAL-B before EVAL exists (§6), the primary arm is frozen at design time
   (§3.3), endpoints and strata are enumerated at freeze; the per-vertical
   Holm family is EXACTLY {retention leg, S2 gate leg, S1 control leg,
   directional-stratum intersection leg}; the two verticals are separate
   registered claims, never pooled. Every integer PASS/FAIL/kill boundary is
   derived by analytic inversion of the exact bounds (never by resampling)
   and pinned in the frozen record before any scored run, per the l3a-parse
   boundary-pinning precedent. The Wilson z=1.645/1.96 forms remain for K=1
   diagnostics only.

Per-vertical independence: family/world and code gate separately; a PASS on one
vertical unblocks ASM-0814 legs on that vertical only [STIPULATED: ASM-0901].

What a PASS licenses — and does not — is fixed by §1's LOAD-BEARING scope line
[STIPULATED: ASM-0900]. What a FAIL means: one registered redesign cycle is
consumed (§7.4); two consumed cycles beyond the first attempt with both
verticals still failing fires K-P3v2(2) [STIPULATED: ASM-0818 restated].

Relation to KOT-DECOMP/1: the scored run exports the per-stage counters the
oracle-decomposition protocol consumes (frame/intent selection, role/direction
binding, entity linking, DSL serialization, execution — rendering is out of
NLB's scope and stays with P3-D-PS/P3-D-ORACLE), so a FAIL localises to a named
stage without a second campaign [STIPULATED: ASM-0821 interface, adopted].

---

item.text: Two hard failures have emerged. First, the worked cost ratios do not follow from the document’s own identity: using its stated 1.7B/360M/135M, ρ=1.4, γ=0.3, r=0.3 assumptions gives roughly 0.34× MONO, not 0.25×; with no shrink it gives roughly 1.04×, not 1.14×. Second, CASC-0’s matched-cost comparator is infeasible as written: a 1.7B model “plus TTC” cannot cost the same as A3 while A3 is also required to cost no more than one plain 1.7B pass, except at the degenerate equality point with no TTC.

item.text:
The architecture is intellectually honest about M2 being load-bearing, but it still gives M2 far more credit than the evidence warrants and CASC-0 does not identify it. My bottom-line judgement: keep CASC as a speculative architecture family, but do not bead CASC-0 as “decisive” in its current form. The present experiment can manufacture an oracle-structured win without showing that structured reasoning permits reasoner shrinkage.

1. M2 is unsupported, with the adjacent evidence leaning negative.

The f2b result supports M4—cheap verify/retry against an aligned deterministic answer key—not M2. It does not show that a small model reasons better in Option-A, nor that Option-A permits model shrinkage. DECONF-A1 makes that distinction sharper: the measured effect survives with only the projected answer key; kernel runtime structure is inert.

The newer f2b-transfer result, still PASS-PENDING-AUDIT, is especially important. It finds a real +0.2507 external-gold lift over the 135M model alone, but `noninferiority_vs_r3` is FALSE. Thus the closest honest-gold measurement gave the shrink headline a live test and did not establish it. That is not a formal refutation of H-CN—there was no internal structured medium—but it is negative evidence against the exact “small plus checking matches large” premise.

nsk1 is also not a direct test, but its 0.76→0.43 result is the only adjacent “supply kernel text to the model” measurement, and it points strongly the wrong way. The fair summary is therefore:

> M2 is untested directly; the verifier mechanism is positive, while both the honest-gold size comparison and the closest text-delivery experiment are adverse.

The design currently presents a roughly balanced uncertainty. The measured balance is not balanced.

The cost model is honest in including F and R, but its arithmetic is wrong. From its own identity and worked assumptions:

- L=1.7B, L′=360M, S=135M, ρ=1.4, γ=0.3, r=0.3, equal input/output lengths gives approximately 0.34× MONO, not 0.25×.
- Setting L′=L gives approximately 1.04×, not 1.14×.

More importantly, “net-negative without M2” is only true at that worked point, not algebraically in general; sufficiently short cores could make M3 carry a saving. It should publish the break-even inequality rather than a categorical statement.

Even that diagnostic understates practical cost. Parameter ratio is not a reliable wall-clock or energy ratio across three serial, poorly batched models. F/R constrained decoding, calibration, retries, training, dialect adaptation, model loading, KV traffic and lifecycle amortisation can easily move rather than remove cost. The full ledger promises to measure these, which is good, but no planning saving should be quoted before that measurement.

2. The boundary-tax product is not a valid identity with the quantities defined.

`ret_F × acc_core × fid_R` is at best the probability of one clean path under strong conditional-independence assumptions. NLB retention is not simply “probability that CASC’s IR-soft dialect preserves the input”; it is retained covered exactness for a particular closed grammar and execution endpoint. NLB explicitly says its pass is not transferable to a consumer with a different output space or distribution. CASC’s open-vocabulary IR-soft gloss is such a different output space.

A proper decomposition needs conditional terms and an off-path residue, for example:

`P(surface correct) = P(F_ok) × P(core correct | F_ok) × P(surface correct | core correct, F_ok) + rescue/error-cancellation terms.`

The correlations are likely large: hard or ambiguous items will jointly hurt F, L′ and R. Conversely, a non-exact F rendering may still preserve enough semantics for the core to answer correctly. Consequently:

- The claimed 14.5% tax is a planning scenario, not a derived architecture handicap.
- The measured 0.42–0.48 means the existing pinned front-ends are unusable for their tested closed tasks. It does not establish an H-CN end-to-end ceiling of 0.42–0.48.
- CASC is correctly blocked on a new front end, but “dead regardless of every other mechanism” is too strong outside those pins and distributions.

3. The outbound gate is conceptually valuable but not yet measurable as specified.

Making rendering fidelity and unsupported rendering first-class is one of the design’s best contributions. But “mechanically diffed against the core’s claim set” only works for IR-hard closed claims and shallow answer extraction. It cannot determine whether fluent IR-soft prose adds an unsupported implication, changes quantification, weakens a condition, or attributes a claim incorrectly. That requires semantic entailment, independent parsing or human adjudication—the very NL problem the mechanical wording appears to avoid.

The gate also needs to evaluate dangerous rendering unconditionally and stratified by core correctness. A renderer may behave well on correct, simple cores and hallucinate most when the core is incomplete or wrong. `fid_R | core correct` misses that deployment-relevant case.

Keep the outbound analogue, but define separate endpoints:

- completeness and contradiction on correct cores;
- unsupported-claim rate over all rendered outputs;
- behaviour on deliberately incomplete, conflicting and erroneous cores;
- exact mechanical scoring for IR-hard claims;
- independent semantic adjudication for IR-soft claims, with inter-rater resolution.

The proposed 0.90/0.02 thresholds are stipulations, not decisive naturalisation criteria. K2 also leaves a broad inconclusive region: the co-primary demands LB≥0.90 and UB<0.02, while the kill only fires below 0.85 or when the dangerous-render LB reaches 0.02.

4. CASC-0 is neither decisive nor properly matched.

Its oracle-inbound label makes it formally NL-wall-compliant as a diagnostic. It does not make it fair or decisive about the cascade.

The central comparison changes too many variables simultaneously:

- A2: 1.7B, NL input, NL output, TTC.
- A3: oracle Option-A input, 360M, verifier, short core, renderer.

A3−A2 therefore mixes model scale, input representation, oracle canonicalisation, checking, output allocation and rendering. A4 removes checking, but still does not isolate the representation-by-scale interaction that constitutes M2. MONO-S is merely co-reported; it does not repair the confounding.

The matched-cost contract is also arithmetically infeasible. A2 is “1.7B plus TTC” at cost equal to A3, while the primary requires `cost(A3) ≤ cost(A1)`, where A1 is one 1.7B pass. Because A2 cannot cost less than A1, these constraints can coexist only at equality with effectively no extra TTC. The purported strong baseline disappears.

The procedural CLUTRR-shaped corpus further flatters A3. Gold dialect inputs generated from covered records, with engine-derivable intermediate structure, are almost the ideal environment for a structured checker cascade. Held-out compositions control memorisation, not the oracle representation, backward authoring, or record-addressing advantage.

Finally, ≤15 GPU-hours is believable for inference, but not for a serious comparison if the 360M reasoner and 135M renderer require dialect training, renderer tuning and symmetric baseline tuning. The document itself calls R untuned and treats a bad result as only a floor. An experiment whose central component can fail without killing anything is not decisive.

5. The unification claim exceeds the evidence.

f2b fits only as an answer-grain aligned-key verify/retry precedent. Moving that mechanism to intermediate reasoning steps assumes that intermediate claims are extractable, addressable, correct-keyed and engine-checkable. None is measured.

NLB supplies design discipline, not literally the same F. Its frozen front end emits a closed grammar; CASC F emits an open IR-soft dialect with IR-hard islands. NLB’s own compatibility rule denies automatic transfer.

CODEVERT is even less supportive: it is explicitly a kernel-free calibrated-parser plus exact-store architecture. Its G0 coverage says something about code extraction and executable substrate, not about Option-A as a neural reasoning language or reasoner shrinkage.

knull may supply a dialect control or prose material, but its scholarly-quality question does not decide whether a dialect is an efficient internal neural representation.

These lines can be interfaces or controls around CASC. They do not jointly constitute evidence for CASC.

6. Issue #12 is only renamed, not answered.

The document admits this, then rhetorically softens it by saying DECONF-A1 “fits” the medium story. That fit is not evidence. An aligned answer key is an external verification target; an internal reasoning language is a computational substrate. Showing the former sufficient at one checker seam says nothing about the latter.

If anything, DECONF-A1 strengthens the generic-dialect/null hypothesis: kernel-specific structure contributed no measured checker decision semantics, and generic typed representations remain viable. “The kernel is non-optional because we define it to be the internal language” resolves optionality by architecture choice, not empirical necessity. The real issue remains whether kernel structure beats a plain canonical dialect after authoring and consumption costs. The current evidence supports no “medium” claim—not even a medium-strength one.

KEEP: the explicit separation of V-C2/V-DEC/V-LL; deferral of invasive variants; the statement that M2 is load-bearing; total-system and lifecycle accounting; the outbound safety seam; the kernel-versus-plain-dialect control; and the G2-oracle/G3-natural/G4-full-frame distinction.

DROP: “CASC-0 is decisive”; the current 14.5% boundary-tax claim; the incorrect worked ratios; “F literally is NLB-FE/1”; the cross-corpus G1 “saving_max” obtained by multiplying CODEVERT/m0b routing shares by f2b’s 0.103 cost ratio; and any suggestion that DECONF-A1 supports kernel-as-internal-language.

CHANGE: update the evidence register with f2b-transfer’s pending-audit result and its failed noninferiority; make the boundary model conditional rather than multiplicative; redesign dangerous-render evaluation for IR-soft semantics; replace the infeasible A2 budget; and describe CODEVERT/f2b/NLB as adjacent components with unmeasured transfer assumptions, not one converged architecture.

The single most important fix is to replace CASC-0 with a factorial M2 isolator. Before testing the whole cascade, run model size `{360M, 1.7B}` × medium `{content-matched NL, Option-A, plain typed dialect}`, score the structured core before any renderer, and separately cross verifier `{off,on}` where affordable. M2 requires a positive size×medium interaction—not merely A3 beating an incomparable NL system—and the 360M structured arm must approach the 1.7B NL arm at lower measured cost. Test R separately afterward. If ≤15 GPU-hours cannot support that identification, call CASC-0 an informative screen, not a decisive experiment.

Epistemically: CASC is plausible enough to retain as a hypothesis. It is not presently evidence-backed enough to promote as the programme’s efficiency architecture, and today’s evidence gives M4 a positive prior, M2 a negative-to-weak prior, and kernel-specific attribution a strongly deflationary prior.
